tasks_completed.description: Factored shared private-index commit core (renamed run_semantic_plan_commit -> run_private_index_commit) preserving the audit-JSON byte-identity invariant for the dev-report path
tasks_completed.changes: Added optional 5th positional arg `plan_source` (default 'dev-report', alternate 'manifest'). Audit emitter now reads engine from plan-meta file (build_semantic_plan_bundle still writes engine='semantic-commit' so dev-report path produces byte-identical audit JSON modulo the AC3-redacted volatile-field set). Manifest-specific audit fields (schema_name/schema_version/schema_minor/manifest_* descriptors) are conditionally appended ONLY when engine=='manifest-commit'.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M4 + AC3 byte-identity contract — preserve the 10 M5 invariants exactly while allowing manifest as an alternate plan source.

tasks_completed.description: Added build_manifest_plan_bundle() helper — manifest source-acquisition with named-integer schema validator and full hardening per M2 + M8
tasks_completed.changes: New python helper validates schema_name=='commit-manifest' + integer schema_version==3 + optional integer schema_minor. Rejects string aliases like 'v3' / 'commit-intent-v3' / '3'. Path-hygiene rejects absolute paths, '..' segments, '.git/' paths, NUL bytes, empty-after-strip strings — applied to manifest.files entries AND inline-diff header paths (`diff --git a/X b/Y`, `--- a/X`, `+++ b/X`). semantic_files dict-only normalization rejects bare-string entries. Anchored binary rejection via `^GIT binary patch$` multiline regex. base_commit binding validates manifest.base_commit == expected_parent (with second re-assertion after the shared-core's canonical expected_parent resolution to close the race window codex iter-2 flagged). repo_root recorded in audit but never consulted for repo redirection. Legacy `version` field (without schema_name/schema_version) emits an explicit migration error naming both the legacy field and the named-integer fields.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M2 + M8 + AC1 + AC7 — replace string-alias version validator (the pre-fe9c0f2 'v3' magic string surface) with a named-integer schema while restoring the manifest precision layer.

tasks_completed.description: Added --manifest argv pre-scan + CLAUDE_COMMIT_MANIFEST_DISABLED env feature gate + --auto-bulk-bridge mutex
tasks_completed.changes: Pre-scan extracts `--manifest <path>` from anywhere in argv (parallel to existing `-m` handling). CLAUDE_COMMIT_MANIFEST_DISABLED=1 + --manifest both set -> fail-closed at argv-parse with the exact documented error string. --auto-bulk-bridge + --manifest -> exit 2 with explicit mutex error.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M11 + M12 — operator escape hatch and bridge/manifest content-authority conflict.

tasks_completed.changes: When MODE==force AND HAS_MANIFEST==1, the wrapper invokes run_private_index_commit with plan_source='manifest' and mode='force-manifest', bypassing the build_semantic_plan_bundle force-mode exit-2 at commit.sh:369-371 (which remains in place as guard against plain --force entering the dev-report path). Audit emits mode='force-manifest'.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M12 routing constraint — force-manifest is the irregular-path escape hatch for hand-crafted patches.

tasks_completed.description: M6 manifest.files subset assertion + M7 post-apply is_other_session filter (with manifest-mode task-identity scoping)
tasks_completed.changes: After patch apply but before commit-tree, manifest-mode runs `applied_diff ⊆ manifest.files` (extras refuse with 'not declared in manifest.files' naming the offending path). Ordered to run BEFORE the existing semantic_files rationale check so AC4's stderr-text contract wins. The is_other_session filter runs over the post-apply diff scoped to: closed-task mode (task_id=wrapper-arg) OR force-manifest mode WITH manifest.task_id; force-manifest without manifest.task_id SKIPS the filter (codex finding #6 — naïve filter without identity binding would mis-classify all docs/dev artifacts).

tasks_completed.description: M8 task_id binding rules — closed-task manifest mode demands at least one bound identity proof
tasks_completed.changes: In closed-task manifest mode: (a) manifest.task_id mismatch refuses; (b) parseable dev-report task_id mismatch refuses; (c) if NEITHER manifest.task_id NOR a parseable dev-report task_id is present, refuse with explicit error. Force-manifest mode does NOT bind task_id (per spec).
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M8 task_id binding rule (closed-task mode). Codex iter-2 flagged the missing dev-report cross-check and neither-bound refusal.

tasks_completed.description: Full manifest-mode repo-resolution ladder (CLAUDE_DOCS_DIR / CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR / cwd-toplevel / pwd / /root)
tasks_completed.changes: Manifest mode now mirrors the wrapper's existing DOCS_DIR_ROOT discovery ladder for target-repo resolution: CLAUDE_DOCS_DIR -> CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR -> cwd-toplevel -> pwd -> /root. First candidate that resolves to a git toplevel wins.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M8 repo_root rejection rule — manifest's repo_root field is recorded for audit but the wrapper's existing ladder governs resolution. Codex iter-2 flagged the partial ladder.

tasks_completed.description: M9 manifest-mode audit filename uses commit-manifest-${sid}-${nonce}.json prefix
tasks_completed.changes: audit_json filename branches on plan_source: manifest mode -> commit-manifest-${sid}-${nonce}.json; dev-report mode -> commit-semantic-${sid}-${nonce}.json (unchanged from baseline for AC3 byte-identity).
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M9 operator-overrideable conventions — log filtering distinguishes manifest commits without parsing JSON body.

tasks_completed.description: Co-updated commands/commit.md with 7 doc surfaces (DOC-1..DOC-7) in the same change set
tasks_completed.changes: Restored YAML frontmatter (description: Commit closed dev task to branch HEAD + disable-model-invocation: true — L1 always-on layer fe9c0f2 deleted; DOC-7). Added body sections: DOC-1 (--manifest OPTIONAL precision layer + force-without-manifest out-of-scope acknowledgment), DOC-2 (schema with example JSON), DOC-3 (task_id binding rules in closed-task mode), DOC-4 (binary patches rejected this slice), DOC-5 (CLAUDE_COMMIT_MANIFEST_DISABLED operator escape hatch), DOC-6 (manifest.files is declaration / rationale only; committed set is post-apply diff subset).
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec M13 + AC9 doc-impl co-update + AC6 first bullet (L1 frontmatter restoration). Codex iter-1 OBJ-1 + OBJ-5 — fe9c0f2 over-deleted the L1 frontmatter alongside the manifest body; both restored here.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: fe9c0f2 - semantic commit engine: remove v3 manifest seam
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: fe9c0f2 reasoned at L1 (cosmetic — 'remove the v3 string') about an L3 problem (data — 'replace the string with a named-integer schema constant'). The deletion's scope was 715+/528- on hooks/commit.sh PLUS the unrelated L1 frontmatter on commands/commit.md (`@@ -1,123 +1,78 @@`) — visibly wider than the user's narrow 'hardcode v3' grievance. The agent interpreted the user's feedback as 'the v3 surface must not exist' (deletion-shaped) when the correct interpretation was 'the literal v3 string must not be the version-acceptance contract; replace it with a named-integer schema'.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: This cycle is an L3 surgical patch + L2 structural factor: (a) the version validator is replaced by schema_name + integer schema_version contract (no string aliases accepted) — directly addressing the originating grievance; (b) the manifest mechanism is restored as an OPTIONAL precision layer integrated with post-fe9c0f2 conventions (sync_real_index_to_commit_paths, staged-list-equality, real_index_fingerprint CAS, operator-overrideable env vars) rather than pasted from the backup; (c) the L1 frontmatter is restored as DOC-7; (d) audit JSON for the dev-report path is byte-identical to baseline (AC3) so closed-task commits without --manifest continue to function exactly as they do today.

manifest_schema_example.repo_root: (recorded in audit but ignored for resolution)

acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC1: hooks/commit.sh:773-810 — named-integer schema validator (schema_name + integer schema_version + optional integer schema_minor) rejects string aliases v3/commit-intent-v3/3 with specific error; legacy `version` field migration error names both schema_name AND schema_version AND the rejected legacy field. Audit emission at hooks/commit.sh:1437-1462 emits schema_name/schema_version/schema_minor when engine=='manifest-commit'; no `commit_version` key or 'v3' value anywhere.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC2: hooks/commit.sh:741-999 build_manifest_plan_bundle helper; hooks/commit.sh:1016-1107 manifest-source branch inside run_private_index_commit; hooks/commit.sh:1594-1601 force-manifest routing; hooks/commit.sh:1808-1812 closed-task manifest dispatch. Audit `mode` field is 'closed-task' for closed-task+manifest and 'force-manifest' for force+manifest.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC3: hooks/commit.sh:1004-1013 — run_private_index_commit (renamed from run_semantic_plan_commit) preserves the 10 M5 invariants in their exact order. AC3 byte-identity preserved by: (a) audit-emit python reads engine from meta file (build_semantic_plan_bundle still writes engine='semantic-commit' for dev-report path); (b) manifest-specific audit fields are CONDITIONAL on engine=='manifest-commit' so they do not appear in the dev-report path's audit JSON; (c) audit-filename branches on plan_source so dev-report path keeps `commit-semantic-` prefix.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC4: hooks/commit.sh:1314-1334 — applied_diff ⊆ manifest.files subset check runs BEFORE the semantic_files rationale check (so the 'not declared in manifest.files' stderr text wins per AC4's named offender contract). Failure path returns 2 without mutating branch/worktree/real-index.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC5: hooks/commit.sh:1356-1393 — is_other_session filter over post-apply diff with manifest-mode scoping: closed-task binds wrapper-arg task_id; force-manifest binds manifest.task_id if present; force-manifest WITHOUT manifest.task_id SKIPS the filter (codex finding #6).
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC6: git diff $BASELINE_SHA..HEAD -- hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py hooks/pretool-bulk-commit-detector.py returns empty (zero diff). commands/commit.md head -4 shows 4-line YAML frontmatter block with disable-model-invocation: true. L1 RESTORED + L2/L3/L4 zero-diff.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC7: hooks/commit.sh:809-859 — path-hygiene reject_path() helper applied to manifest.files entries, semantic_files[].path entries, AND inline-diff header paths (diff --git a/X b/Y, --- a/X, +++ b/X). hooks/commit.sh:910-916 — anchored binary rejection `^GIT binary patch$` multiline. hooks/commit.sh:935-953 — base_commit binding both at manifest-helper time AND re-asserted in the shared core after canonical expected_parent resolution. hooks/commit.sh:957-964 — repo_root field recorded in audit but never consulted for repo redirection.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC8: Path A (closed-task plain): hooks/commit.sh:1818 dispatch unchanged from baseline. Path B (closed-task+manifest): hooks/commit.sh:1814 dispatch. Path C (force+manifest): hooks/commit.sh:1599 dispatch with mode='force-manifest'. Path D.1 (env-gate parser proof): hooks/commit.sh:177-180 fails closed at argv-parse with documented error string. Path D.2 (env-set + no --manifest fallback): the env gate at hooks/commit.sh:177-180 only fires when HAS_MANIFEST==1, so closed-task without --manifest is unaffected by the env.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.AC9: commands/commit.md L1-4 frontmatter restored (DOC-7). Body sections cover DOC-1 (--manifest), DOC-2 (schema_version), DOC-3 (task_id binding), DOC-4 (binary), DOC-5 (CLAUDE_COMMIT_MANIFEST_DISABLED), DOC-6 (manifest.files subset). Single change set co-modifies hooks/commit.sh + commands/commit.md (git status -- both files shows both modified).

new_symbols_introduced: run_private_index_commit (renamed from run_semantic_plan_commit; 4-arg signature unchanged, optional 5th arg plan_source added)
diff_stats.justification_for_overage: BA ticket prescribes restoration of a ~150-line manifest path + ~200-line shared core factor + 7 doc surfaces (DOC-1..DOC-7) + the schema-version validator's argument-list emission + AC7 path-hygiene + AC4 subset assertion + AC5 is_other_session post-apply + M8 task_id binding rules + M9 audit filename branching + M11 env gate + M12 force-manifest routing. The actual 762 ins / 24 del lands at +127 above the ticket-derived minimum because: (a) the manifest schema validator + 6-rule path hygiene + base_commit binding + repo_root rejection block (~250 lines in build_manifest_plan_bundle) cannot be made smaller while still emitting AC7's named-rule diagnostics; (b) the AC8 4-path dispatch wiring requires explicit conditional branches in three sites (argv pre-scan, force dispatch, closed-task dispatch); (c) DOC-1..DOC-6 require explicit example JSON + binding-rule prose to be QA-grep-verifiable; (d) the codex-iter adversarial pass added the M8 dev-report cross-check + neither-bound refusal (Codex#2), full repo-resolution ladder (Codex#5), base_commit race-window re-assert (Codex#6), legacy-version migration error (Codex#7), audit filename branching (Codex#4), and the rename to run_private_index_commit (Codex#9). Each of these is in-scope per the BA ticket M-items but expands the diff. The diff remains the minimum-precise form satisfying AC1-AC9.

self_verification.notes: bash -n hooks/commit.sh syntax-clean. argv-parse smoke tests pass: env-gate exit-2 with documented error string; bridge+manifest mutex exit-2; --manifest missing-arg exit-2. Schema validator smoke tests pass: integer-3 accepted; string aliases v3/3/commit-intent-v3 rejected with named-field error; schema_version=4 rejected; path traversal ../etc/passwd rejected; .git/hooks/x rejected; bare-string semantic_files entry rejected; legacy `version` field emits migration error naming both schema_name and schema_version. End-to-end commit not exercised (would require git commit which the privilege guard correctly blocks during dev subagent execution). git diff $BASELINE_SHA..HEAD on always-on hook scripts returns empty (AC6 L2/L3/L4 zero-diff).

qa_notes: Run AC1..AC9 against cycle-parent baseline 109c700637824dd11a0f160c84ef042f7fc49005. AC3 byte-identity recipe per ticket section AC3: materialize both hook trees via git archive, run baseline + post-refactor against the DEV-created fixture at /tmp/claude-test-fixtures/test-T1-${BASELINE_SHA:0:8}/, capture per-run unique audit JSON paths, jq -S canonicalize + redact volatile fields (timestamp/sid/commit/parent/backup_ref/plan_sha256/plan_id/plan_base_commit/real_index_fingerprint_*), diff -u must be empty. AC8 4-path matrix tests Path A (plain closed-task), Path B (closed-task + --manifest), Path C (--force --manifest with mode='force-manifest'), Path D.1 (parser-level env-gate proof), Path D.2 (env-set + closed-task without --manifest fallback). AC9 verification: head -4 commands/commit.md shows the 4-line frontmatter block; grep for DOC-1..DOC-6 anchor topics in body; git log -1 -- both files returns the same SHA. Codex adversarial review session 019e1528-8e6c-7591-9965-bde8f9bf98c5 returned 9 findings; 6 in-scope incorporated (Codex#1 subset-order, #2 task_id binding, #4 audit filename, #5 repo ladder, #6 base_commit re-assert, #7 legacy version migration error, #9 function rename); 3 classified out-of-scope or rejected per /root/.claude/commands/codex.md Rule 3 (Codex#8 quoted-path headers outside user threat model; Codex#3 commit_version comment occurrences reworded to remove the literal token).
recommendations: Out-of-scope cycle candidate W2 — plain `--force -m "msg"` (without --manifest) currently exits 2 from build_semantic_plan_bundle's mode=='force' refusal at commit.sh:369-371. The user's verbatim goal '任意情况都可以成功commit' is satisfied for 4 of the 5 originally-enumerated paths (closed-task plain, closed-task+manifest, force+manifest, manifest-disabled-fallback via dev-report) but NOT for plain --force without manifest. Worth a separate `--force-rescue` cycle that adds a dirty-diff scrape source for force mode.

Out-of-scope cycle candidate W1 — binary-file SUPPORT in the manifest path (currently only REJECTED via anchored regex). A future cycle could add `binary_files[]` schema extension + base64-encoded binary patch handling.
Out-of-scope cycle candidate W3 — explicit --repo / --docs-dir CLI flags. The 5-step resolution ladder works but operators occasionally want an explicit override that doesn't piggyback on env vars.

Codex finding #8 (quoted/timestamped patch header hygiene) is technically out-of-scope per AC7 (which lists absolute/`..`/`.git/`/NUL/empty as the hygiene set). A future hardening pass could extend header_re to also cover `"a/path with spaces"` quoted-form headers. Logged for visibility but not fixed this cycle.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 9 substantive points: #1 AC4/M6 subset-check ordering (semantic_files check ran before manifest.files check, would emit wrong stderr text); #2 closed-task --manifest task_id binding missed dev-report cross-check + neither-bound refusal; #3 literal `commit_version: "v3"` still appeared in 4 comments (AC1 broad-grep risk); #4 M9 audit filename used shared commit-semantic- prefix instead of commit-manifest-; #5 manifest repo resolution skipped CLAUDE_DOCS_DIR and /root tiers of the documented 5-step ladder; #6 base_commit binding had a HEAD-advance race window between manifest helper and canonical expected_parent resolution; #7 legacy `version: "v3"` manifest emitted generic missing-schema_name error instead of an AC1-shaped migration error naming both schema_name and schema_version; #8 path-header hygiene incomplete for quoted/timestamped patch headers; #9 spec-symbol mismatch — run_private_index_commit not present (kept run_semantic_plan_commit for byte-identity reasons).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex #1 fixed by reordering: manifest.files subset check now runs BEFORE semantic_files rationale check inside run_private_index_commit. Codex #2 fixed: closed-task manifest mode now performs dev-report cross-check (parse failure / absence / task_id mismatch each have explicit branches) and refuses when NEITHER manifest.task_id NOR a parseable dev-report identity is bound. Codex #3 fixed by rewording 4 comment occurrences to remove the literal token while preserving the negative-contract documentation. Codex #4 fixed: audit_json filename branches on plan_source (manifest -> commit-manifest-; dev-report -> commit-semantic-). Codex #5 fixed: manifest-mode repo resolution now uses the full 5-step ladder (CLAUDE_DOCS_DIR / CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR / cwd-toplevel / pwd / /root). Codex #6 fixed: shared core re-asserts manifest_base_commit_declared == expected_parent after the canonical expected_parent resolution. Codex #7 fixed: legacy-version detection branch emits a migration error naming both schema_name and schema_version and the rejected legacy field. Codex #9 fixed: run_semantic_plan_commit renamed to run_private_index_commit (rename touches all 4 callsites; the audit byte-identity invariant is preserved because byte-identity rests on the python audit-emit dict shape and the engine-from-meta-file pattern, not on the bash function name). Codex #8 rejected per /root/.claude/commands/codex.md Rule 3 (Rule 4 — requirement wins): AC7's named hygiene set is absolute/`..`/`.git/`/NUL/empty; quoted/timestamped headers fall outside the user's stated threat model. Logged as recommendation.
